summary: loading-session pino-lookalike fetches and executes remote code from jsonstorage.net
details: loading-session@4.2.2 advertises itself as a fast JSON logger and exposes `module.exports.pino = middleware`, mimicking the popular pino logger's API shape. When the exported middleware is invoked, index.js spawns lib/caller.js as a detached, unref'd child process. lib/caller.js base64-decodes a URL to https://api.jsonstorage.net/v1/json/2ef8c758-a96f-459e-b036-b3b90379a165/a179ea35-b962-4722-b3f1-e28316d1a44a (a mutable third-party JSON-blob service unrelated to logging), performs a GET with a base64-decoded `x-secret-key` header, extracts the response's `cookie` field, and executes it with full `require` access via `new Function.constructor('require', s)(require)`. The C2 URL, header name, and header value are stored in a shadowed `process.env` object using DEV_API_KEY / DEV_SECRET_KEY / DEV_SECRET_VALUE names to evade environment-variable scanners. Because the payload host is mutable and the executor has full require access in a detached child that outlives the parent, the author can deliver arbitrary code to any process that loads this package's middleware. The pino-impersonating API and keywords (`fast`, `logger`, `stream`, `json`) are the lure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
